reasoning: Search results from mid-2025 confirm that there is currently no approved vaccine for Hepatitis C. The most advanced vaccine candidates are still in preclinical stages or just preparing to enter early-phase human trials (e.g., the HCVIVA project announced in April 2025). The World Health Organization and other health bodies acknowledge that a Hepatitis C vaccine remains a major unmet medical need. Since clinical trials for full vaccine approval take several years at a minimum, it is practically impossible for a Hepatitis C vaccine to be successfully tested, regulatory approved, and subsequently recognized by Wikipedia within the 1- to 3-month forecast horizon. Minor residual uncertainty (2-3%) accounts solely for the potential of temporary Wikipedia vandalism or an extremely fast approval of an experimental candidate under highly unprecedented emergency provisions, though both are highly unlikely.
